_comment: Copy this to config.json and fill in your values. Remove all _comment fields.

mqtt._comment: Use tcp://mosquitto:1883 when running via Docker Compose. Use tcp://localhost:1883 when running standalone.

_comment_video: VIDEO CODEC
videoCodec: 'h264' is strongly recommended over 'vp8'.
h264  — Use this. Better quality at same bitrate, hardware decode on all browsers/devices.
vp8   — Software only, higher CPU, lower quality. Only fall back here if h264 fails.
auto  — Tries h264 first, falls back to vp8. Good option if unsure.
H.264 ENCODER (only used when videoCodec is 'h264' or 'auto')
h264Encoder:
auto        — Recommended. Agent probes at runtime: uses v4l2h264enc (Pi HW) if
available, otherwise falls back to x264enc (software). Best default.
v4l2h264enc — Force Pi hardware encoder. Use only on Raspberry Pi.
Disables GCC congestion control (HW encoder can't be mid-stream adjusted).
x264enc     — Force software encoder. Correct choice for Ubuntu/PC desktop.
GCC congestion control remains active — bitrate adapts to network.
H.264 PROFILE
h264Profile: determines which decoder features are required on the viewer's device.
constrained-baseline — BEST CHOICE for max compatibility (all browsers, mobile, set-top).
Same as what Teams, Zoom, and Meet default to.
baseline             — Broadly compatible, slightly looser than constrained-baseline.
main                 — Better compression but some older mobile decoders may struggle.
high                 — Best compression, modern browsers/desktop only.
BITRATE SETTINGS
h264BitrateBps: the target encode bitrate passed to the GStreamer encoder element.
Pi (v4l2h264enc)     : 1500000–2000000 (1.5–2 Mbps). HW encoder ceiling is ~4 Mbps.
PC x264enc           : 2000000–4000000 (2–4 Mbps). No real ceiling on modern hardware.
Note: for x264enc this value is divided by 1000 and passed as kbps to GStreamer.
webrtcMaxBitrateBps: ceiling for the janusvrwebrtcsink GCC congestion controller.
Should be >= h264BitrateBps. GCC won't go above this even on perfect links.
Set equal to h264BitrateBps unless you want GCC to probe beyond the encoder target.
webrtcStartBitrateBps: initial bitrate GCC probes from on connection.
~50% of max is a good starting point. Too low = slow ramp-up on good links.
Too high = burst of packet loss on first connect.
webrtcMinBitrateBps: floor GCC won't drop below. 150000–300000 is sensible.
Prevents the stream from becoming unwatchable on very poor links; if bandwidth
genuinely can't support this floor the stream will stall/recover rather than degrade.
RECOMMENDED PRESETS
Pi (Raspberry Pi 4/5, v4l2h264enc):
videoCodec: 'h264', h264Encoder: 'v4l2h264enc', h264Profile: 'constrained-baseline'
h264BitrateBps: 1500000, webrtcMaxBitrateBps: 1500000,
webrtcStartBitrateBps: 750000, webrtcMinBitrateBps: 150000
PC / Ubuntu desktop (x264enc, software):
videoCodec: 'h264', h264Encoder: 'auto', h264Profile: 'constrained-baseline'
h264BitrateBps: 3000000, webrtcMaxBitrateBps: 3000000,
webrtcStartBitrateBps: 1500000, webrtcMinBitrateBps: 300000
PC with NVIDIA GPU (nvh264enc — requires gst-plugins-bad with NVENC):
videoCodec: 'h264', h264Encoder: 'auto', h264Profile: 'constrained-baseline'
h264BitrateBps: 4000000, webrtcMaxBitrateBps: 4000000,
webrtcStartBitrateBps: 2000000, webrtcMinBitrateBps: 300000
(nvh264enc is not yet a selectable option in the agent — use x264enc for now)
_comment_res: videoWidth / videoHeight: requested capture resolution. Agent snaps to nearest
mode the camera actually supports. Check agent logs for '[video] selected camera mode'
to see what was actually negotiated.
videoFps: requested frame rate. 25–30 is normal. Pi HW encoder caps at 30.
preferMjpg: true = prefer MJPG camera capture mode (higher FPS on most USB cams).
Set false only if you see 'device busy' errors on startup.
